claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) reflects a label-register vocabulary of fewer than 150 distinct content words, with a word-frequency distribution that fits a truncated power law rather than full Zipf, consistent with a glossary or label list rather than running prose.
evidence_for: Zodiac section entropy 3.7149 bits is the lowest across all sections
Zodiac section contains only 1,297 words over 12 folios, consistent with short labels around diagrams
Label-heavy sections would produce reduced vocabulary diversity and lower entropy
Belief weight 0.197-0.207 already supports label-oriented encoding in zodiac section
evidence_against: Low entropy could also result from heavy repetition of cipher padding or null glyphs rather than label structure
Cosmological section (10 folios, 2,199 words) has higher entropy despite similar illustrated context
test: Fit a truncated Zipf/power-law model vs. a full Zipf model to the unigram frequency distribution of zodiac-section words; compute vocabulary growth curve (Heaps' law) for zodiac vs. recipes section; measure type-token ratio and compare against herbal (largest prose candidate) and recipes sections.
test_metric: Heaps' law exponent for zodiac section; type-token ratio; goodness-of-fit (AIC/BIC) for truncated vs. full Zipf model on zodiac word frequencies
test_threshold: Confirmation: zodiac Heaps' exponent < 0.4 (vocabulary plateaus early, consistent with closed label set) and truncated Zipf fits better (lower AIC). Denial: exponent > 0.55 and full Zipf fits better, suggesting running prose with normal vocabulary growth.

reasoning: If the zodiac section is confirmed as a closed-vocabulary label register, it becomes the highest-priority decipherment target because label content (month names, zodiac names, star names) is predictable, enabling crib-based attacks on the cipher.
